channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for. — 131 replies — 524.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

Open thread • Replies: 131 • Updated: 2026-06-23 13:51 UTC

OP

If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

LINK is a $5.7B token on $57.5M of annual fees. That’s ~99x revenue, and it has been flat for years, straight through every “banks are coming” cycle. The promise was always that adoption converts into value. It hasn’t. It’s been “soon” since 2020.

So here’s the question no diehard wants to answer:

What fee number, by what date, would make you sell? If LINK still earns ~$57M in 2028, is the thesis wrong, or is it still “soon”? If you can’t name the number and the date, you’re not investing in Chainlink. You’re believing in it. And belief has no exit.

Early replies (first 3)

62382825
never. Its not about the link token, its just about the hope for the future it gives. That is what keeps me going, even if its a delusion. Thus I will never sell, because as long as I hold the token that fantastical delusion can still be real in my mind

62382842
chatgpt post

62382990
>>62382842
OP is a mentally ill faggot

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62396292
>>62396263
whatever you do dont sell... but please keeep buyin it, i stilll have 8k link in a cold wallet i need to get in new york.

duming it next bull run when link hits 14 dollars and youre euephoric,


just like how you were euephoric ehen link was 27 this bull.

62396331
>>62382813
I believe in Chainlink. Now what fud cuck AI copypasta poster?

62396341
>rBIjPT2t
this retard is literally in every thread, desperately begging anons to keep holding until x catalyst, which is ALWAYS something 6-12 months away, and once that deadline finally arrives, he IMMEDIATELY pivots to a brand new carrot, which is again, 6-12 months away
>pls guise DON'T SELL, this year is OUR YEAR!1!!
every
fucking
year
and he's been doing this since 2020
I'm not even sure if he's paid or legit retarded at this point

62396355
>>62396233
>>62396238
>they removedd the vid
lol yeah, they removed the vid just like they removed "all references to staking v1.0" topkek
here it is: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7eCrqQs6RiI

Now timestamp where she said this.

62396362
>>62396341
>gets BTFO with all his different fud IDs
>fires up another new ID just to cry like a bitch

tale as old as time

62396382
jeet marines been really quiet since this dropped

https://youtu.be/26N4u0ihIT4?t=12
https://x.com/taodaily_io/status/2064680225577804267

62396420
>s-s-samefag
>w-w-why do you care???
>y-y-you're crying, not me
the insecure little fagit always accuses of you of samefagging, always thread stalks at least 3-4 different threads, and he does that for almost 10-12 hours 4-5 days per week, it is insane really
he took a small hiatus at the beginning of the year, when sentiment was utter dogshit and he hadn't found the new deadline to shill yet, but then returned at full force and has been going nonstop ever since
I'm still not sure if this is a case of legitimate mental ilness or paid shilling, because I honestly fail to see the value of aggresively shilling LINK on /biz/ of all places, unless the team is in ultra desperation mode

62396430
>>62396355
upload the full onne that was 3 hours long, this is the vid where i got my screenshot, '
sergey was on the right side...

this is from the same day tho...

ccant timestamp it since chainlink cut it out.

62396574
>>62396420
It takes a few dozen posts to get as triggered as you are right now, meaning you clearly just switched IDs.


>>62396430
According to this fuddie thread https://warosu.org/biz/thread/S61013113, the "15 years" bs came from this talk: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=x_D1MM_0EGQ
Go ahead and try to find it in there.
She says something vague about production being "a long ways away" in that talk, but somehow the fuddies turned that into 15-20 years.

62396650
>It is literally an entrenched jewish rent seeking project minted on a completely arbitrary blockchain which INCREASES frictions intentionally for no reason to collect a fee, and it is totally unnecessary and 100x slower and more expensive than its competitors to use.
At this point there are years worth of docs, threads, talks and even memes explaining this stuff. You don't have to like LINK, but at least understand what you're criticizing instead of throwing random FUD at the wall and hoping something sticks.
Price action is trash, yes. The team dumps tokens, yes. LINK has been a painful hold with massive opportunity cost. There is no denying that. But you should seriously decouple price FUD (totally valid) from tech FUD (makes you sound retarded).

62396660
>>62396650
>still emphasizing WORDS
>moar larp
funny how you're hitting every thread lmao
back up that 100x figure on cost and speed, faggot

62397187
>>62396650
>But you should seriously decouple price FUD (totally valid) from tech FUD (makes you sound retarded).

comical. what will it take for you to realize you don't have a piece of ownership of the tech company?

62397245
>>62396341
>>62396420
What about >>62395971
>>62396233
verbose xrp shill and adem in every single thread? these guys claim to not hold the token and yet have been spamming the board for years now, adem since 2021, verbose xrp shill seems like he really showed up around 2023 or so but hard to say for sure. Any thoughts on them? Wouldn't you say its more pathetic to strictly post fud in threads of tokens you don't even own?

62397794
>>62397187
>what will it take for you to realize you don't have a piece of ownership of the tech company?
I realize that.
Chainlink is a cybersecurity company, the security being driven by cryptography and economic incentives. Without the token the entire thing is not better than https. If these peopld truly wrote code for over ten years, only to build useless tech that already exists in other form.
Cryptography is nothing new. Cyprography applied in a decentralized manner, with economic incentives (staking/slashing) is innovative and revolutionary.
If it really is a scam, then it was a very elaborate and convncing one, and I will be willing to admit I was retarded and wrong. If it's not a scam and my thesis is correct then a lot of people on this board will rope. I don't really care if you buy it or not. I don't know or care about you, and you definitely don't have enough money to move the needle on the price.
All I am asking you is to simply take a week and study the protocol before making nonsensical claims.

62397819
>>62397794
>Chainlink
>Decentralized
Lmao. Go run a node and try to get whitelisted to actually being able to get it used and then come back to us.

62397938
>>62397819
>1pbtid
>phoneposting with capitalzations of acronyms
Hi verbose xrp shill I see you are hopping around id's in this thread.

62398220
>>62397819
>Chainlink
>Decentralized
Decentralized does not have to mean the cypherpunk fantasy of "banks aren't needed and everyone is a sovereign individual." It's largely a matter of semantics.
Decentralized simply means that the nodes participating in the network are independent of one another.
If I tell you to watch a football game and report the result back to me in exchange for money, it's difficult to guarantee that you're telling the truth.
If I tell you and another person—whom you don't know—to watch the same game and report the result back to me, my chances of learning the true result increase, because the two of you would have to find each other, coordinate, and risk not getting paid.
Now imagine I tell 20 people to do the same, and on top of that, each person must deposit $1,000 to be eligible to participate and earn money. Nobody has an incentive to lie, because doing so would put their deposit, any future payments for their service, and their reputation as football-game reporters—all of which generate income for them—at risk. That's a decentralized oracle network.
This is the meaning of decentralization and what Chainlink is building - consensus without a chain. Consensus as a service.
I'm done spoon feeding for today. Hope that was clear and helpful to all the perplexed and confused anons here.

62398292
>>62398220
Is McDonald's decentralized because they have 5 employees that I can ask what the price of a big Mac is?

62398491
>>62396382
"token holders don't get any revenue (pause for the clips...) at this point"
>at this point
it's a trick.

62400097
>>62397938
Hi biepum, monitoring the threads I see.
Hows that flippening doing?

62400234
>>62400097
>samefagging fuddie with a fresh new ID complaining about linkies being in link threads

What the fuck is wrong with you.

62400297
>>62400234
>Every post that triggers me is made by the same poster.
Mind breaking heavy bags

62400329
>>62400297
You really have no idea how insanely obvious you are, do you?

62400348
>>62398292
kek
the entire thesis of Chainlink btfo in a single post

62400559
>>62400329
You are deranged lmao, its hilarious how you think you are always talking to 1 person.

62400655
>>62400559
This is a dead board, this thread has been up for almost a week now.
Yet you keep actively seeking out Link threads and then crying about all the "Link shills" you see in them. All the while pretending it's a normal thing to do.

It's not, and you stick out more than ever.

62400679
>dead board
>retarded shill keeps necro bumping his dead shill thread
>hurrrr why does the entire board shit on me?? you're all samefags1!!1!1
stop giving him (You)s and don't forget to sagepost frens

62400691
>>62400679
>shill thread
oof, might wanna check out the OP, samefagboy.

62400897
>>62400655
I just noticed you spamming the thread bieps, just hopped in. Hit you with the reminder of the flippening. You verbally shit yourself again. The regular.

62400962
>>62382813
>white male

no thanks

OP

NO NO NO NO NO NO!!!!!!!!!!

THIS SIMPLY CANNOT BE! ELON MUSKRAT IS A CONMAN, SPACEX MERELY A PONZI SCHEME!!!

SOMEONE MUST PUT A STOP TO THIS NOW!!!!!!!!!!!! REDDIT ARMY RISE UP!!!!!!!!!

62375140
this is entirely sustainable, you have EDS if you disagree

62375150

Short it then, stupid nigger

62375163

>run by the first trillionaire

62398144

62398150

62398178

YOU SAID THIS WAS MY TICKET OUT OF THE PERMANENT UNDERCLASS

It's a hoot and a holler as far as i'm concerned

62398185

62398188

62398204
>>62381795

62398211
>>62398185
>Collapse the dollar
>Now everyone is a millionaire :)

62398213
>>62381795
wtf is mann._michael13 on about?

62398226
bros im worried OP will tie the noose after getting pumped and dumped, we need to be much more reassuring to our friends that mistakes happen and we can overcome mistakes if we work together. Don't let op be another hanged man in the bathtub meme.

62398877
>>62392029
I hate the French

62398970
>>62398090
This animu was a fun watch. Hope you enjoyed it too schadenfreude bro

62398978
>>62375128
>2pbtid

62399080
>>62375150
Bet.

Guess my gains.

62399090
>>62375128
You were saying?

62399160
>>62375140
Well, think of it like this. The right half is all the economic activity that having a jetliner enabled for humanity. Space is vaster still than the air. Space can do what air can do, and more, we can get resources in space. So you have to imagine that the total economic potential of space can be as great if not greater than the economic activity from air travel.
Spacex is the closest of all companies to making it so that the biggest cost to sending something to space is just the fuel to do it. Can they achieve that? If so, then they have a the jetliner of space travel. It would take tens if not hundreds of billions, and at least a decade if not 2 or 3 to replicate what spacex has done, from scratch. Most of their competitors aren't starting from scratch, but they are way behind. If you wanted it done quickly, it would be very expensive, and perhaps not even possible to buy the capability that spacex presents itself capable of delivering.

62399168
>>62375128
The one cow village of OP.

62399196
>>62375128
It is absolutely uncanny how /biz/ calls the top like this.
Anons like OP are like a inverse oracles.

62399203
>>62399196
not really it was kinda obvious
its priced like a top 10 company but makes no money. Even it does start to make top 10 company profit the price wont go anywhere so yeah

62399214
Rip to all the jeets falling for elon scam. I guess your villages are going to starve in the next few months

62399292
>>62399203
Yeah you could expect spacex to go down, but the timing is always tied to gloat posting.
>OH NO NO NO
>YOU’RE SO DUMB FOR BEING BEARISH
>HA HA HA HA
You could almost set puts to this.

62399317
SPCX will drop to at least the IPO price in the near term. I wouldn't even consider buying it until that happens.

62399364
>>62375784

>but you don't want to make it up into outer space

62399387
>>62375128

>>62398185

62399575
>>62399160
Your first mistake was assuming spacex is a space company.

62400470
this is unironically going below 50. Retards who bought this and were shilling it here for a month finally got what they deserved. Even my fucking bank was shilling this scam to me. Remember it's better to miss out on too many oppotunities than buy too many scams

62400488
>>62375163
I love how lefties constantly do shit that nerfs their prosperity. ESG, DEI, Elon bad etc etc

62400495
>>62400488
hello did you look at the chart recently?

62400827
>>62398185

62400854

item.title: Irl crime — 50 replies — 200.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Irl crime

Open thread • Replies: 50 • Updated: 2026-06-23 13:49 UTC

OP

Share crime stories and currently relevant opportunities ITT. IRL shit, not online scamming.

Inb4
>muh bankers muh politicians
Wow, very funny, what an original joke

Early replies (first 3)

62393319
>>62393307
I fucked your mom.

62393322
12 years ago my first job was in a supermarket. I randomly decided to steal a candy bar for no reason, but i felt so guilty that I confessed to my supervisor a day later and offered to pay. She said it was okay, and nothing happened.

62393326
>>62393319
This guy paid me $50 so he could fuck your mom.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62396109
>>62393424
Nice one. I bet that happens all the time.

When I was in high school in Minecraft everyone wanted shrooms but there was no source for them so I started ordering Aminita muscaria online and sold it to a dealer in bulk. He bought bags of it from me every week for about five weeks and then finally told me my shit sucks and people wanted their money back. I still made thousands and in high school that’s a lot.

62396291
>>62393307
I've unironically heavily considered card fraud considering how easy it is to do and the fact that literal gangbanging hood negroes do it regularly and get at worst a mild punishment for a fraction of the money stolen
I just don't want to get raped and glock dookied in prison so I'm too scared to commit

62396405
>>62395828
How the fuck did you not have mold contaminate the crates??I struggled endlessly with this back in the day

62396415
>>62396291
Pussy

I knew quite a few lads that went into that and made a buck

62396427
>>62396415
>lads
the US is soft on cc fraud but the UK is even more so desu
I know your fraud yutes over there rarely even get time but here they'll get a year or two at least so the government can pretend they're doing something

62396438
>>62393307
I eat poop

62396596
>>62396427
Jail is ok

62396641
>>62396405
it is difficult to keep contamination out but I would do things to mitigate like using a lot of isopropyl alcohol to clean surfaces, using a glove box for inoculation, etc. I do think when you spawn seed to pasteurized manure is the part that a real roll of the dice. just so many opportunities to introduce contaminants there.

62396900
>>62393356
>I meant torturing someone for crypto, armed robbery etc
There are two reasons you'd be this up front about such topics here
>honeypot trap
>low civilization tier subhuman

62396921
This thread glows

62397020
>>62396900
I'm a low civilization tier subhuman. I don't give a fuck about your gay "civilization.

62397200
>>62393307
When I was a young teenager, I had a friend who bought a bb gun pistol. He then waited for evening, went to the park, hid behind the bushes near a path, and then jumped out at old people walking and stole their wallets and jewelry. He made a lot of money. He asked me to join in, but I wasn't interested.

No idea what happened to him. Probably dead. Good riddance.

62397518
Bump

62397557
>>62394022
What a literal fucking nigger. Repent

62397565
>>62396900
Retards posting obviously fake quotes is always funny

62397959
>>62397557
Kys

62398384
>>62393307
Me on the picrel

62398550
>>62393307
>>62393356
>>62394022
>>62395812
>>62397020
>>62397565
>>62397959
Izzat farmers
Abandon thread

62398943
Bump

62399147
>>62393307
Sleep Tight Pigger

62399469
This one time my bros and i knew about 35 mil worth of gold bars so we painted the floor beneath their safe with explosives and then had a decoy boat drive off as we cracked the safe in the water below. Then we scuba'd the bars to a seperate boat, and wbile celebrating one of our own bros decided to double cross us as we celebrated our heist. Fuckin loser didnt even know what to do with all that money besides copy all our ideas. So anyway we come up with a plan to steal the gold back by sending our dead safe cracker's daughter (whom the coward was unfamiliar with) to sneak into his house disguised as a television internet repair woman, and we cut the lines of his tv. Our plans were almost ruined when his neighbors had a house party so we called it off and had to rethink our plans; we surveilled him and found out he was moving our gold in 3 trucks so we modded 3 mini coopers all souped up and pulled another demolished floor trick, the loser viewing from the helicopter couldnt see because we had a billboard truck that was blocking the view of the gold truck falling into the subway below. We knew which truck the gold was in because the ride height and our hackerbro friend basically led them right into a traffic jam above the explosive floor. After incapacitating the drivers we cracked the safe and transported the gold in our minis to a train. Our former betrayor tried to buy his way onto the train only to find that we paid off the mobsters that owned the train a ton of cash and gold to be part of our plan too. Then we listened to money by pink floyd.

62399612
>>62399469

That hackerbro's name? Albert Einstein.

62399967
>>62393322
I one time worked at Walmart a summer in high school and someone was cutting up bags to eat handfuls and I had to clean it. They didn’t give me a lunch break because I was so new and I was throwing away dozens of bags so I ate a single chip in a bag that was popped but not fully opened. Got fired on the spot as they were watching me on video like a hawk. Fuck that gay company. I’ve never bought from them since

62400055
>>62399967
Lol did you learn your retard-goy lesson?

62400082
>>62393319
Bestiality is technically illegal

62400093
I have not and will commit any crime as I am a model citizen and do not break the law.
Anything I have posted on this site that contradicts that is fairy tales and only a fool would take those such posts as fact.

62400095
>>62400093
Will NOT commit*
God fucking damn

62400096
>>62400093
Wow that's really clever and original and funny

62400920
>>62393307
comfy

62400948
>>62393307
You call that a crime scene?
Here’s a real big one bed in the street you lightweight!

item.title: CZ is richer than Bill Gates — 22 replies — 88.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
CZ is richer than Bill Gates

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-06-23 13:54 UTC

OP

And the 19th richest person in the world. You're seriously trying to tell me crypto is dead?

Early replies (first 3)

62398500
>>62398496
yes? that's pretty much all stablecoins

62398527
>>62398500
No faggot, it's because he owns 90% of a $100B crypto exchange. Plus whatever else

62398541
>>62398496
It's dead because everyone but the exchanges are gone. They just spoof volume and trade between themselves whenever they come up with any new bullshit narratives. MUH privacy, MUH dexes, MUH quantum, etc.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62398554
>>62398527
yea, and of all exchanges, binance has the biggest stablecoin reserves ($50 billion). at least half his wealth is not crypto

62398577
>>62398541
100% this, and it’s what I’ve been saying for a long time. Just look at all the top tokens and they all have 9+ figures in daily volume. The whole market is manipulated. It’s all fake and gay. Anyone who argues otherwise is a desperate bag holder screaming into the void. No one is coming to save them

62398601
>>62398554
CIRCL technically has 75 billion in stablecoin reserves but only has a 20 billion dollar valuation. Keep saying dumb shit, it makes me feel smart

62398606
>>62398601
circle doesn't own all of their aum silly. binance does own their stables. but you bring up another point that valuing binance with a hard number is a retarded exercise

62398652
>>62398500
> so you're telling me a digital dollar is just a dollar with extra steps and no fdic

62398705
>>62398496
Nigger sold everyone’s bitcoin and killed the altcoin season. He deserves nothing. The only justice would be him going bankrupt buying back all the bitcoins he sold that wasn’t his.
Scamnance wasn’t even subtle about it either. Fuckers sold all of the crypto and had them all go to 0 literally. For weeks people couldn’t withdraw from scamnance because they didn’t have the coins. Scamnance should be wiped off the face of the earth. Piece of shit scam exchange.

62398935
>>62398496
Scammer. Dont cex!

62398946
>>62398541
Everyone went onchain, check ethereums stats!

62398950
>>62398946
Hence the atl cex balance

62398957
>>62398496
Cexes are so 2018! Fuck cz and all the leeches!

62399007
I dont believe this for one second

62399038
>>62399007
That he sold "your" coins for stabkes? Do a binance wintermute googling then!

62399095
>>62398496
He got rich shorting crypto kek

62399719
>>62399095
Liquidating fags with user fund movements to wm and back plus scamming with shitcoin orwcles like on 10/10 and many more jewish tricks.

62400048
>>62398606
>valuing binance with a hard number
Coinbase's market value is what provides a price point for Binance

62400079
damn billy hella broke

62400316
>>62398496
>Chingchong Zhao is a CCP puppet
>Bill Gatesberg is a US puppet
Big deal

62400412
>>62399038
No, that he has more money than billy. Either hes exaggerating or bill is hiding shit

62400992
>>62398935

item.title: Why aren't you holding BTC? — 17 replies — 68.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren't you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-06-23 13:52 UTC

OP

Too expensive? Prefer alts? Don't trust it? Or do you simply think there are better opportunities elsewhere?
Curious to hear your thoughts.

Early replies (first 3)

62395695
you can get better gains on stocks. those companies work for your money. btc sits there doing nothing but has the same risk profile as sharing a hobos needle without greater fools left to carry it to $200k

62395915
give me reasons to hold btc.

62395925
I am holding because I bought in 2020 before etfs now I refuse to sell because I'll get audit raped by the CRA supposedly so I'm stuck holding until it's got better tax laws or borrowing rates.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62396002
Invest only in actual products made by actual companies.

62396879
>>62395695
People have been calling Bitcoin worthless for over a decade. At some point it's worth asking why demand keeps returning every cycle.

62398057
>>62395915
Because I can hold it for the long term while exploring new ways to put it to work.

62398163
>>62398057
Might as well do a bank transfer as it's more private, costs less and the money won't disappear into thin air.

62398819
>>62395655
Because it's not actual Bitcoin, unlike Bitcoin SV (ticker: BSV), which I'm holding.

62399676
>>62398819
BSV is super dead super super dead

62399898
>>62395655
BTC is dead.
Western governments are all over it, they want your Capital Gains via taxes.
Unless you got in early and got out of the country with your gains early you’re fucked. Australia in its recent tax changes has hidden a trap for crypto gains, by stating any earnings made overseas in the last 12 years must be retroactively given to the government to be taxed, if you’re audited and your physical wealth exceeds your earnings, you’re fucked.
Thats outside that statute of limitations for records. It’s a dual purpose instrument- to catch people working in tax havens like Dubai who claimed they were leaving Australia forever then returned 10 years later with unexplained millions, but also to catch crypto investors who were using decentralised exchanges. The ATO has been using bots to crawl through ledgers linking transactions to addresses and piecing together expenditure and entry. If you’re caught it’s not avoidance it’s evasion.

It’s too late now but y’all should’ve moved over to XMR years ago.

62400285
>>62399898
Btc is dead? Wow. Who are you?

62400293
>>62398057
>>62398163
How!!!???

62400366
>>62395655
Worst opportunity by every metric.

62400842
>>62396879
Why do people go to the casino?

62400859
>>62400285
Epstein, Jeffrey Epstein.

62400895
>>62395655
microstrategies fucked it up. he's so maxdebt and overleveraged it's too easy to rip his company apart with the next recession and then take his btc for pennies on the dollar

62400974
>>62400895
also notice the ceo for mstr is white male. we know the results already

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 137 replies — 61.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 137 • Updated: 2026-06-23 11:44 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62393786
Unfortunately I misquoted, this anon
>>62378439

Is who said it best in regards to this post
>>62393776

62393801
Please keep all stock suggestions relevant to the discussion at hand

62393834
>>62392256
the same but at least im locked in. If I lose my job tomorrow and never invest another penny I'll still be fine to retire well before 65. that's peace of mind. 10 more years and my rentals will all be paid off and that will more than cover my existence costs.

>>62393449
>just make money
>just buy assets
yeah that's how 99.9% of everyone with actual money got it. biz loves to talk about how they made x billion on cumcoin yet in the real world thats akin to winning the lotto

62394075
LASR
IPGP
PLAB
QUBT
RGTI
IONQ
Nbis
IREN
AAOI
POET
SIVEF
SOITEC
SMHN
CRDO
ALMU
LASE
OPTX
SKYT


Some stock picks to look into in the world of photonics

62394345
>>62394075
>NBIS ($286.69)

-Too expensive for us

>CRDO ($271.83)

-Too expensive

>AAOI ($161.85)

-Too expensive

>SOITEC (Euro stock, $133.39)

-Too expensive

>IPGP ($118.01)

-IPG Photonics primary supplier of high-power fiber lasers and automated laser systems heavily utilized in Elon Musks TESLAs

>LASR ($64.73)

- **Laser Photonics (LASE)** – Develops industrial laser systems and is expanding into **anti-drone technology**."
>IREN ($59.96)

-Not relevant, disregard


>IONQ ($56.55)

-Moreso quantum related, not really photonics

>SKYT ($36.57)

-Recently bought by IONQ,


>RGTI ($21.36)

>>Reddit says:
-the most speculative, revenue is actually declining and the P/S is hard to defend at any timeline, the honest framing for all of them is these are binary outcome bets not compounders. fault tolerant quantum is 5-15 years out, they'll keep burning cash and diluting until then.

>POET ($12.15)

Pump ended

>QUBT ($10.76)

-They make the tlfn photonic chip. Where silicon based chips use electrons, their tfln chips use
photons to send information. First tfln chip foundry in the U.S.


>SIVEF ($10.12)

-Fidelity only, pushing to get US LISTING


>LASE ($2.32)

-We missed this pump

>OPTX ($12.11)

- We missed this pump

LYTE - Releasing August, from the same company that brought you DRAM.

FOTO - this is the ETF for photonics tickers, very over diversified however

62394373
>>62373752
Is there a market for books with alternative physics?

62394789
The third topic shall be future tech and science new

62394870
>>62392312

Go learn how to create a validator.

PulseChain validators right now are raking in around 10%. It's nice.

62394942
>>62394345
>too expensive
anon, fractional shares exist

62395063
>>62373752
>I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.
Yes, going to college or vocational school and getting a higher paying job is probably the correct path for the vast majority of you.
>t. 32yo engineer making $135k
Go for STEM and if you can't cut it then pick a trade that interests you. If I didn't make it in STEM I was considering being an Air Traffic Controller.

62395577
Brainstorming

Software created current-state AI, AI created current state demand for hardware resources, hardware surge created demand for production materials, hardware costs contribute to higher cost datacentre build costs, running the datacentres with current state AI running on current state hardware consumes higher energy demand.

>AI - priced in
>Hardware - priced in
>Materials - priced in
>Datacentres - priced in
>Energy - priced in

So the disruptor to this ecosystem might be sitting where we started - software development. The race to highly energy-efficient AI models is the next logical thing. The current trajectory is unsustainable from a resources perspective, and the financial burn rate is unsustainable.

You make AI consumption highly resource efficient, all of those things come down as the demand reduces. Or does supply just increase to fill the increased capacity?

That takes me to the next innovative era that will consume the increased capacity;
> Robotics and humanoids / androids - building interest but probably not fully priced in yet
> Transport and automated movement - AI powered, driverless vehicles (short Uber)
> Highly efficient reusable rocketry - transport anything anywhere quickly (reusability powered by AI robotics)
> Personal digital self's - Consumer facing AI-version-of-you running your life, in your house, ordering your groceries - your life on autopilot (think a personal JARVIS)
> medical application - localised AI models in hospitals and medical rooms - control robotics in surgery
> mobile devices - uncertain future, at some point the personal handheld device model may come to an end

So my take away for a macro, strategic tech play could be to accumulate software and tech players with resources that can produce highly efficient AI models that use less resources - Microsoft is trading at a discount? - instead of over-committing to hardware and the fundamentals. Model efficiency could improve at any stage.

Thoughts?

62395589
>>62394870
Don’t you need like 7 figs worth of pulsechain to be a validator

62396733
>>62377801
Why would you want those with over 10K to leave? It sounds like you could use some advice especially if you're five figures and "investing" in trading cards. Your finest minds have more than 10K because they minimally are employed with decent jobs.

62398741
>>62395577
I agree with your thesis that we should accumulate up and coming software players with great ai

that said

> Robotics and humanoids / androids - building interest but probably not fully priced in yet

we're too early

> Transport and automated movement - AI powered, driverless vehicles (short Uber)

https://www.azfamily.com/2026/01/08/waymo-passenger-flees-after-car-drives-phoenix-light-rail-tracks/

I live in AZ, everyone hates Waymo. Uber drivers will literally bully the crap out of them


>Highly efficient reusable rocketry - transport anything anywhere quickly (reusability powered by AI robotics)

Too broad

>Personal digital self's - Consumer facing AI-version-of-you running your life, in your house, ordering your groceries - your life on autopilot (think a personal JARVIS)

This has to be inevitable by the end of the next decade. The writing is on the wall, I mean think of Siri and Alexa, different in scope but has easily prepared consumers to accept the eventuality of "you" just running your own tasks. Small business owners would also love this. Imagine a traveler who stays at an Airbnb month long for business and uploads his AI to manage the boring stuff of his new "home" while he acclimates to the business and travel of the area.


>medical application - localised AI models in hospitals and medical rooms - control robotics in surgery

this has potential

>mobile devices - uncertain future, at some point the personal handheld device model may come to an end

We're too early

62398767
>>62398741
https://www.eurekalert.org/news-releases/1132599

>Publishing in the Journal of the American Medical Association (JAMA) online June 22, the study team found that online portal messages more than doubled between 2020 and 2025 (by 153 percent). By contrast, total telephone calls decreased by 6 percent over the same period.

>“Clinical staff will need to be trained in mastering the tools of messaging in healthcare; in using AI support programs, including chatbots that can frame content to minimize its complexity; and in making the most effective use of clinician time needed for online billing and online counseling,” said Dr. Segev, who is also a profession in NYU Grossman’s Department of Population Health.

>Already, he noted, NYU Langone uses AI support tools to speed up drafting of physician and provider notes.


Doctors already use google to diagnose people

62398789
Some topical reading for today, gentleman, nothing too difficult takes less than 5 mins. Lets take this slow and not bite off more than we can chew

62398798
>>62396733
They'd have no reason to frequent and appreciate these threads or this company. You think rich boomers understand our circumstances?

62398799
Is diversification a meme? Thinking it's better to just go all in on a single stock than an index fund when you have a small account.

62398806
Page 2, come on anon, it doesn't hurt to learn the basics, i'll go over with you, together

62398809
>>62398799
How much are you putting down? I'm thinking of doing $1000 all in picks and making a folder that way

62398813
There, lets read over these three

62398868
>>62398799
You are better off getting a job

62398876
>>62398868
https://voca.ro/1aI6fGYlNRNN

62398905
>>62394942
Don’t get that either. Even if you only bought full shares how could you not afford 200 dollars worth of shares? Is this under 10k net worth or under 100$ net worth

62398985
>>62376699
> you're arguing with a script and losing

62399017
>>62380691
bump

62399272
>>62398789
>Wavelength (λ) (THE SYMBOL IN HALF LIFE)

Distance between repeating points of a wave (e.g., crest to crest). Determines frequency and, for light, color.

>Amplitude:

Maximum height of the wave. Determines intensity (brightness for light, loudness for sound).

>Phase

Position of a wave within its cycle. Determines how waves add or cancel when combined.

>Polarization

Direction of oscillation of a transverse wave. For light, it is the orientation of the electric field.

62399358
Tomorrow we shall give predictive programming a try as photonics will likely be a difficult venture

62399394
10k- networth reporting in! You guys are too smart for me with all those grand ideas.

62400367
>>62398789
tl;dr

item.title: VOO and Chill — 13 replies — 52.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-23 13:54 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62400564
>>62400560
>RICH
lol good one anon
you may be ok, but you won't be rich

62400620
>>62400564
What makes one rich then, can only doing a good trade here and there achieve this
And depending on the definition, being “OK” probably puts you in the 85th percentile versus people in credit card debt at least
I’ve considered trying to be more sector specific or something but idk

62400633
>>62400620
when you are passively producing about 200k in cash annually. that allows you unrestricted living in any part of the US. you need at least 4 mil+ folio to do that comfortably.

62400636
Thing is, index funds are for either:
A) People who have a lot of money where % changes magnify in a meaningful way
B) People who start early enough in life and consistently enough for compounding to mean anything

The problem is we have people starting at age 35 with like 150 a month thinking it's going to somehow mean something.

>b-but something is better than nothing!
Yes and I'm sure that used car and maybe a holiday you can buy with it when you're 65 from cashing out all your investments will be great.....

62400638
>>62400633
You can that with VOO and chill though…

62400645
>>62400638
>5% withdrawal rate

YIKES
DESU you need 5mil, and you aren't getting there without constantly invest invest invest and wa

62400651
>>62400645
*waiting and hoping you don't die along the way.

62400652
>>62400554
ngmi

62400699
>>62400652
Exactly you can’t explain it
>>62400636
Well I’m 26 and have low expenses so I’ve been DCA my salary into VOO
I guess at 26 I could have more risk but idk this is hitting 20% returns after a year and a half good enough for me

62400995
Qqq is just more narrow and carries more risk. It’s true that s&p and nasdaq carry many of the same companies right now, but nasdaq can crash and fail to recover, or growth can move to another segment of the economy. S&P will adjust when either of these happen. Nasdaq will be Nasdaq. It seems if you invest in both that’s fine, but you are putting your money in the same place twice and increasing risk.

62400473

Crypto was invented to do sneaky transactions behind the backs of foreign governments they were spying on. Yet they seem to catch common criminals trading all the time. And confiscate Iranian Bitcoins easily.

62400498
>>62400398
Crypto peaked in 2021. All those BLM arsonists and Ukrainian agents needed to be paid. Ukraine war starts and suddenly hardly new money is being pumped into crypto. Trump gets in and reins in on the Deep State and it comes crashing down. Not just Trump and friends enriching themselves. Unlimited fed money stopped going into the pot.

Stocks are banker shit
Boomer rocks and LATAM real estate are the escape

62400776

>Strategic bitcoin reserve is just strategy's bitcoin reserve. Literally change two letters.

item.title: How am I ever supposed to get married and start a family if 95% of people are horrible with money? — 16 replies — 11.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How am I ever supposed to get married and start a family if 95% of people are horrible with money?

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-06-23 12:31 UTC

OP

How am I ever supposed to get married and start a family if 95% of people are horrible with money? The last girl I dated was nice but 4 months into the relationship mentioned having thousands in credit card debt and student loans for a degree she didn't even finish. The girl I dated before that never had any savings and was always impulse buying stupid shit. I worked hard to stay debt free and save money. I don't want to pick up the tab for someone just for them to divorce me anyway. Don't give me some BS about its my duty to be a "male provider." I just feel like I'm never going to find someone who ticks my other boxes and isn't a financial retard landmine.

Early replies (first 3)

62399691
>>62399687
>I just feel like I'm never going to find someone who ticks my other boxes and isn't a financial retard landmine.
Then do not date?

62399745
>>62399687
dont get married. its a meme, and even if they aren't financially insolvent, they can simply divorce your ass whenever they choose and confiscate your wealth.
consider any other contract. imagine signing a contract where if the OTHER PARTY breaks the contract, they are entitled to rewards paid by you. does that sound fair? do you think that would be a good idea? because that is modern marriage.
https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=5e2vFc1AIOA

62399750
>>62399687
just dont care about money, then there's no problem

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62399764
No idea. I'm considering just becoming a passportbro.

62399766
>>62399687
It’s really hard to find someone good enough to have kids with, that’s for sure. My advice is always to essentially network your way into a decent lady. Make friends with decent people and successful couples, and then ask if they know anyone

62399813
>>62399687
Precisely why it should be easier.
>except for homosexuals
>nobody is proud of you

62399831
>>62399687
You marry a bitch richer than you, retard.

62399869
>>62399745
I didn’t have much to be taken from me and my wife makes much more money than me and more retirement savings so that’s why it was worth the shot of preserving the white race and breeding with a western woman. It’s not easy though scrubbing the indoctrination bros, but it’s the cross I chose to bear. I also have her on board in the event she ever feels the need to divorce (even though I don’t consider that an option and you make it work one way or the other), that she wouldn’t get a jew lawyer involved because it would only negatively impact and steal from our kids future

62399889
retards like you deserve to lose everything they have, you never learn, it's like you are going through life willfully blind to everything happening around you, then you come complain on a site like 4chan about it lmao

62399903
>>62399889
How did you know I was retarded?

62399913
>>62399687

62399925
>>62399913

And a sack

62399989
>>62399869
>scrubbing the indoctrination
You're literally indoctrinated yourself what the fuck do you mean saving the white race you're a random retard and cleetus is gonna have 10 times the white babies you'll ever have, all with room temp IQ from their alcoholic mother, stop posting in pol faggot

62400016
>>62399687
Sounds like you want a gay man partner to fuck your boy pussy.
>why women be women?

62400520
>>62399687

62400535
>>62399687

item.title: What's the value? — 3 replies — 10.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
What's the value?

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-06-23 13:41 UTC

OP

So many people don't like to drink pure water. Tasteless isnt fun enough.
Who would pay for it when you can drink Coke, Fanta, fruit juice?

Those people ask:
" WHAT'S THE VALUE ? "

They dont see the value because they always had access to drinkable water. They never experienced real tirst. They cant understand what it means to not have water.

Those same kind of people will ask:
"What's the value of Bitcoin?"

They cant understand.

Early replies (first 3)

62400617
>>62400585
How fat are you that water doesn't taste good?

62400629
>>62400585
There should be weight restrictions to post here

62400872
>>62400617
Right lmao water is fucking amazing op must be a nigger

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

62400364

Follow her home and keep texting her and fucking her as tyronigger to make her think shes cucking you. When the baby is ready to pop out you accompany her to the hospital dressed as mancoon and much to her surprise it comes out pure white despite her only fucking your niggbull persona for the last 5 years. Rip off your mask and take a fat truthdump in her mouth that shes been reverse-cucked.

if she still insist, get a PI and let him follow and picture the cheating event!

OP

62396376

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62396717
>>62396251
ah fuck we're going lower aren't we?

62400718
>>62396717
You're asking the wrong questions. The right questions are
1. How long until Bitcoin doubles
2. How long until Bitcoin 10xes

item.title: Polkadot update — 10 replies — 5.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot update

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-23 11:58 UTC

OP

ITS HERE

XRP, ETHEREUM AND LINK IS COOKED

https://medium.com/polkadot-network/dot-dao-and-the-need-for-jamkb-a069e72e9728

https://news.openshore.io/web3-summit-2026-recap.html

Early replies (first 3)

62399118
5. Privacy is a Competitive Advantage
In a world of surveillance capitalism, Polkadot's emphasis on privacy-by-default and user agency differentiates it sharply from other ecosystems. The proof-of-personhood protocol and private payment demonstrations showed concrete technical approaches, not just rhetoric.

Do they think big Google and Facebook will let them get away with this?

62399184
After further research

It seems we have a fork of consensus

https://forum.polkadot.network/t/introducing-a-new-jam-token/13029

https://medium.com/polkadot-network/dot-dao-and-the-need-for-jamkb-a069e72e9728

So what the fuck is JAM and how will it bring value back to DOT marketcap?

62399190
>The JAM protocol presupposes the existence of a token to ensure soundness in its state usage. I argue that DOT would make a highly suboptimal choice and that a new, fixed-issuance token to represent the new Polkadot resource of JAM state makes the most sense. Assuming DOT DAO were to launch a Polkadot JAM for its upgrade, then this new resource-access token would be wholly owned by DOT DAO by default. DOT utility would continue as at present, implying control of DOT DAO — and with it, effective ownership of the Polkadot JAM — as well as the means for staking, collateral and coretime payment within the Polkadot ecosystem.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62399197
>>62399190
oh boy, another scam

62399251
Slurped

62399395
>>62399197

Linkie crying seeing another crypto vibing

62400350
>>62399184
a new resource is being introduced to Polkadot with the JAM upgrade (fast shared storage like RAM) and you need one JAMKB per KB of RAM you want to use on the network, so you can't buy more tokens than there are KBs of RAM

62400419
>>62399084
DOT will make us all rich beyond our Wildest dreams!!

item.title: Crypto-Hopium — 28 replies — 83.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Crypto-Hopium

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-06-23 13:38 UTC

OP

>The CLARITY Act is about to be signed.
US corporations will begin buying large quantities of Bitcoin and altcoins.
>The US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve will accumulate 5% of the total supply using a rebalanced Strategic Gold Reserve as collateral.
Trump is about to audit Fort Knox, and ARMA is being drafted to prevent future presidents from selling for a minimum of 20 years.
>The housing market has introduced cryptocurrency backed mortgages.
Participants will hold their cryptocurrencies for 15 and 30 year terms.

Early replies (first 3)

62393159
>The CLARITY Act is about to be signed

really nigga? I thought this shit was dead for this year

62393165
>>62393089
Clarity Act isn't going through. It's completely stalled in negotiations and they've got a week to get something like 10 dems to cross lines.

62393203
>>62393165
According to whom?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62393204
>>62393089
Funny how you niggers always desperately need a meme date/event/regulation/narrative, this board is 99% indians who cannot think for themselves.

62393377
>>62393204
It's funnier how you can read the OP and disregard it as if it has zero positive implications for the cryptocurrency market.

62394871
>>62393089
The government won’t buy anything until everyone capitulated nigger. I’m almost certain it will dump and a final capitulation will begin.

62394904
>>62393203
the recess begins on june 29 and runs to july 10. after that they once again have only 20 days to pass it before the august 1 recess that goes into september, which is midterm season.
>10 dems
the bill does have opponents and it isn't filibuster-proof without some dems voting it in. plus it needs to be bipartisan or it's going to be doa regardless. every dem that has signaled support has done so contingent on some kind of concession, few of which have been addressed

62394929
>>62393089
Trump was meant to be the crypto president and the first thing he did when he took office was rugpull his followers with a shitcoin, ruining whatever goodwill crypto might have had at that point. It's not happening. It's over.

62395050
>>62393204

we need the banks to buy the coins that kill the banks

it's easy, see?

62396159
>>62393089
Cryptobros just don't understand that 10/10 was curtain call for crypto

62397222
>>62393089
What kind of drooling retard actually thinks clarity will make crypto pump?

You are convincing NOBODY

62397241
>>62396159
cryptobros have been exterminated. I think the only people still remotely bullish are orthodox cycle believers.

62397263
>>62397241
>>62396159
crypto will be dead for at least another year. Maybe bitcoin can get back over 100k in 2028, but its dead in the water for many months to come. Imagine holding crypto since last year when you could have been getting rich in the stock market. Sorry crypto bros. Its over. And YOU LOST.

62398703
>>62397222
drooling retard here...
I do

62398770
>>62397222
>What kind of drooling retard actually thinks clarity will make crypto pump?

Anon, it gives banks the greenlight and all the compliance laws they need to start messing around with a bunch of crypto tech they've been developing for years.

62399414
>>62393089
I don't understand, the clarity act doesn't help bitcoin in any way. Only shitcoiners care about this getting passed.

62399467
>>62393203
Well I've found Eleanor Terret useful on the act, she's the fox news crypto correspondent or some shit and has good white house insight. She called it passing out of the house accurately but is saying it's currently just dead and won't make the july date.

62399527
>>62398770
>Banking Cryptotech
>Look inside
>It's just SPAXX but on a blockchain
You retards really are getting hyped for fucking money market funds. This is almost as embarrassing as the euros that brag about their high yield savings account yield.

62399531
>>62399527
>mfw they invented a worse way to do what your bank already does

kek, enjoy your 4% with extra steps anon

62399584
>>62393089
I hope so /biz has been a massive bottom signal lately.

62399617
I really feel like it won't pass. No one cares about crypto right now.

62399643
>>62399617
> crypto is dead until it isn't. you're just early to the next cycle.

62400286
>>62393089
Balack Obama

62400375
>>62393377
That's because we comrehend the shapes formed by these "alphabet" " letters".

62400681
>>62399527
the new Banking market is not sexy but it's a more efficient, more reliable way of the doing the same old same old. They want that guarantee and the speed that blockchains offer. The other market that will blow up is the NFT market. Now the banks will be selling NFTs in contrast to everyone selling NFTs in 2020. They have assets that are properly represented on the blockchain and any trade will be honored.

62400720
>>62393165
Nah buddy, I have direct lines here. The votes aren’t the issue, that part is sealed. Timing is still tight though. The industry is putting major pressure on right now, it’s pretty likely to go through.

62400761
>>62400681
>erm yes teller, I’d like to buy a picture of a monkey smoking a banana wearing sunglasses as an investment.

Want to know how I know you smoke crack?

62400852
>>62400761
You realize what NFTs can do besides art, right? UNIQUE IDENTIFIERS. You can have a house deed, your social security card and benefits, ANYTHING that is unique "tokenized". Any sort of business financial product can be made into a token or an NFT. If youve ever been a liquidity provider to a dex like Uniswap/Sushi/etc, you'd realize why NFTs are a big deal. The monkey pictures were just a proof in concept hype meme trend for normies. But "boring" everyday status quo items being tokenized and NFT is a hugely big deal. Huge markets coming up out of nowhere several months after the Clarity Act is finally law! You need to DYOR newfren. (Unless youre just memeing of course)
